_comment: INTENDED branch-protection rules for 'main'. Authoritative configuration lives on GitHub; this file is the reviewable intent that `scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh` pushes to the API. A discrepancy between this file and live state is either a pending manual apply OR drift — resolve by re-running the script.
notes: Only one required status check: `Build + structural + links + Playwright`.
Code review + Security review moved to LOCAL subagents (see CLAUDE.md 'Local review workflow'); not CI-gated. Reactivation triggers listed in .github/workflows/tests.yml header.
enforce_admins=true means even the repo admin is subject to these rules — bypass requires deliberate toggle-off.
required_approving_review_count=0 because Ghislain is sole admin; the integrity gate is Ghislain manually clicking Merge.
allow_force_pushes=false on main; feature branches can be force-pushed.
To apply this file to live branch protection, run: `scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh` (Ghislain-only; my client-side hooks block the underlying gh api PATCH/PUT/DELETE call).
The script pipes `jq '.rules'` into `gh api PUT .../protection --input -` — the raw file is NOT API-shaped (has wrapper keys like `_comment`, `notes`) and would 400 if passed directly.

Sequencing when changing required_status_checks: apply this file to the server BEFORE merging the PR that removes the old check names from workflow YAML — otherwise main is wedged because the server still expects the old checks that no job emits. See PR #15 for the first time we hit this.
